articles.title: Rochdale Hornets Suffer Heavy Defeat to Midlands Hurricanes
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Hornets faced a 42-4 defeat against Midlands Hurricanes at Avery Fields, marking their fourth consecutive loss in all competitions.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Hornets fell to their fourth successive defeat in all competitions, losing 42-4 to Midlands Hurricanes at Avery Fields on Saturday. The result leaves the Hornets struggling to regain form after a three-week break from competitive action.

The match began poorly for Rochdale, with former player Lewis Else scoring for the Hurricanes within five minutes. The Hornets thought they had equalised shortly after when TJ Boyd crossed the line, but the try was disallowed for stepping into touch.

Midlands extended their lead with tries from Luis Roberts and Jon-Luke Kirby. Despite some promising moments, including Jamie Dallimore's kicking game putting pressure on the Hurricanes, Rochdale could not convert their chances into points. The home side continued to dominate, with Else adding another try and Aidan McGowan scoring just before half-time, giving Midlands a commanding lead.

In the second half, James Woodburn Hall scored twice for the Hurricanes, while Rochdale came close to scoring when George Roby lost possession as he attempted to ground the ball. The Hornets managed a consolation try from Dan Nixon late in the game, but it was not enough to change the outcome. Rochdale now turn their attention to an important fixture against Sheffield Eagles next week, where a victory is crucial for their playoff hopes.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.excerpt: Recent articles from Hartlepool Mail and Kildare Now provide insights into the current status of Rochdale Hornets and their upcoming fixtures.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Hornets have been highlighted in recent updates published by Hartlepool Mail. The articles detail the team's current status and upcoming fixtures.

The latest news covers the Hornets' performance and any significant changes within the team. Supporters are encouraged to read these updates for the most accurate information.

In addition to Hartlepool Mail, Kildare Now has also reported on the Hornets, offering fans a variety of sources for the latest information.

These articles serve as a useful resource for those following the Hornets and wishing to keep track of the team's progress and developments.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.
articles.right_to_reply: Anyone directly affected may request a correction or right of reply by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.
